templates.name: AWS Lambda by HTTP
templates.description:
The template is designed to monitor AWS Lambda by HTTP via Zabbix, and it works without any external scripts.
Don't forget to read the README.md for the correct setup of the template.

You can discuss this template or leave feedback on our forum https://www.zabbix.com/forum/zabbix-suggestions-and-feedback

Generated by official Zabbix template tool "Templator"

items.name: Get alarms check
items.description: Check that the alarm data has been received correctly.

triggers.name: AWS Lambda: Failed to get alarms data
triggers.event_name: AWS Lambda: Failed to get CloudWatch alarms for the Lambda function
triggers.description: Failed to get CloudWatch alarms for the Lambda function.

items.name: Async events dropped sum
items.description: The number of events that are dropped without successfully executing the function. If you configure a dead-letter queue (DLQ) or an `OnFailure` destination, events are sent there before they're dropped.

items.name: Async events received sum
items.description: The number of events that Lambda successfully queues for processing. This metric provides insight into the number of events that a Lambda function receives.

items.name: Async event age average
items.description: The time between when Lambda successfully queues the event and when the function is invoked. The value of this metric increases when events are being retried due to invocation failures or throttling.

items.name: Total concurrent executions
items.description: The number of function instances that are processing events. If this number reaches your concurrent executions quota for the Region or the reserved concurrency limit on the function, then Lambda will throttle additional invocation requests.

items.name: Dead letter errors sum
items.description: For asynchronous invocation, the number of times that Lambda attempts to send an event to a dead-letter queue (DLQ) but fails. Dead-letter errors can occur due to misconfigured resources or size limits.

items.name: Duration average
items.description: The amount of time that your function code spends processing an event. The billed duration for an invocation is the value of `Duration` rounded up to the nearest millisecond. Duration does not include cold start time.

items.name: Errors sum
items.description: The number of invocations that result in a function error. Function errors include exceptions that your code throws and exceptions that the Lambda runtime throws. The runtime returns errors for issues such as timeouts and configuration errors.

items.name: Get Lambda alarms data
Get metrics data
items.description:
Get Lambda function metrics.
Full metrics list related to the Lambda function: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/monitoring-metrics.html

items.name: Invocations sum
items.description: The number of times that your function code is invoked, including successful invocations and invocations that result in a function error. Invocations aren't recorded if the invocation request is throttled or otherwise results in an invocation error. The value of `Invocations` equals the number of requests billed.

items.name: Get metrics check
items.description: Check that the Lambda function metrics data has been received correctly.

triggers.name: AWS Lambda: Failed to get metrics data
triggers.event_name: AWS Lambda: Failed to get CloudWatch metrics for the Lambda function
triggers.description: Failed to get CloudWatch metrics for the Lambda function.

items.name: Throttles sum
items.description: The number of invocation requests that are throttled. When all function instances are processing requests and no concurrency is available to scale up, Lambda rejects additional requests with a `TooManyRequestsException` error.

items.name: Unreserved concurrent executions maximum
items.description: For a Region, the number of events that function without reserved concurrency are processing.

discovery_rules.name: Lambda alarm discovery
discovery_rules.description: Used for the discovery of alarm Lambda functions.

item_prototypes.name: [{#ALARM_NAME}]: Get metrics
item_prototypes.description: Get metrics about the alarm state and its reason.

item_prototypes.name: [{#ALARM_NAME}]: State

trigger_prototypes.name: AWS Lambda: [{#ALARM_NAME}] has 'Insufficient data' state
trigger_prototypes.description: Either the alarm has just started, the metric is not available, or not enough data is available for the metric to determine the alarm state.

item_prototypes.name: [{#ALARM_NAME}]: State reason
item_prototypes.description:
An explanation for the alarm state reason in text format.
Alarm description:
`{#ALARM_DESCRIPTION}`

trigger_prototypes.expression: last(/AWS Lambda by HTTP/aws.lambda.alarm.state["{#ALARM_NAME}"])=2 and length(last(/AWS Lambda by HTTP/aws.lambda.alarm.state_reason["{#ALARM_NAME}"]))>0
trigger_prototypes.name: AWS Lambda: [{#ALARM_NAME}] has 'Alarm' state
trigger_prototypes.description:
The alarm `{#ALARM_NAME}` is in the ALARM state.
Reason: `{ITEM.LASTVALUE2}`

config.label: Access key ID
config.description: Access keys are long-term credentials for an IAM user.

macros.description: ARN assume role; add when using the `assume_role` authorization method.

config.label: ARN assume role
config.description: ARN for the assume role when using the assume_role authorization method.

macros.description: Add when using the `assume_role` through instance metadata or environment authorization method. Possible values: `false`, `true`.

config.label: Assume Role Metadata Authorization
config.description: Chose `True` when use instance metadata service to retrieve role credentials.

config.label: API response timeout
config.description: Timeout for API responses in seconds.

macros.description: The Amazon Resource Names (ARN) of the Lambda function.

config.label: Lambda function ARN
config.description: The ARN of the Lambda function.

macros.description: Filter of discoverable alarms by name.

config.label: Alarm name filter (matches)
config.description: A regex to filter discoverable alarms by name.

macros.description: Filter to exclude discovered alarms by name.

config.label: Alarm name filter (not matches)
config.description: A regex to exclude discovered alarms by name.

macros.description: Filter of discoverable alarms by namespace.

config.label: Alarm namespace filter (matches)
config.description: A regex to filter discoverable alarms by namespace.

macros.description: Filter to exclude discovered alarms by namespace.

config.label: Alarm namespace filter (not matches)
config.description: A regex to exclude discovered alarms by namespace.

macros.description: Sets the HTTP proxy value. If this macro is empty, no proxy is used.

config.label: HTTP proxy
config.description: HTTP proxy to use for requests.

config.label: AWS region
config.description: The AWS region where the Lambda function is located.

config.label: Secret access key
config.description: Secret access key can be retrieved only at the time you create it.

macros.description: Region used in assume role request.

config.label: STS region
config.description: The AWS region for the assume role request.
